title: delicate smoked middle-eastern smoked haddock harees
description: a crispy, jammy, and adventurous middle-eastern dish made with smoked haddock, served in a copper pot with a side of toasted flatbread.
instructions: heat the olive oil in a large copper pot over medium heat.
add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute.
add the cumin, coriander, paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
add the rice and water to the pot, and bring to a boil.
reduce the heat to low, cover the pot, and simmer for 18 minutes.
while the rice is cooking, heat about 1-2 inches of oil in a deep frying pan to 350f.
fry the smoked haddock fillets until crispy and golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
drain the fried haddock on paper towels and set aside.
once the rice is cooked, fluff it with a fork and add the toasted flatbread on top.
serve the crispy fried haddock on top of the rice, garnished with chopped fresh herbs, if desired.
